LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CELLCO PARTNERSHIP'S (DBA VERIZON WIRELESS) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3 BECAUSE THE SINGLE INCIDENT IS INSUFFICIENT TO PROVE A HOSTILE WORK ENVIRONMENT AND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THAT SHE IS DUE ANY FURTHER COMPENSATION OR PENALTY AMOUNT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OTHERWISE DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS MET HER SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN OF PRESENTING EVIDENCE FROM WHICH A JURY COULD FIND DEFENDANT'S LEGITIMATE EXPLANATION PRETEXTUAL. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Amended Notice Of Motion To Allow Pltf To Conduct Discovery Into Deft Cellco Partnership, Dba Verizon Wireless Financial Condition
Off Calendar May-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Andrea Londono's Motion To Allow Plaintiff To Conduct Discovery Into Defendant's Financial Condition is denied. Plaintiff has failed to show that there is a substantial probability that she will prevail on the punitive damages claim. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT CELLCO PARTNERSHIP, D B A VERIZON WIRELESS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3 BECAUSE THE SINGLE INCIDENT IS INSUFFICIENT TO PROVE A HOSTILE WORK ENVIRONMENT AND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THAT SHE IS DUE ANY FURTHER COMPENSATION OR PENALTY AMOUNT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OTHERWISE DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS MET HER SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN OF PRESENTING EVIDENCE FROM WHICH A JURY COULD FIND DEFENDANT'S LEGITIMATE EXPLANATION PRETEXTUAL. Moving party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/AY

Notice Motion Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2000.00
Off Calendar Jan-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Elizabeth C. Bendana, Esq., appeared via telephone for Plaintiff and moving party: Andrea Londono.
Charles Thompson, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Verizon Wireless
HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED with modification: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 23, 24, 41, 43, 44, 52, 56, 57, 58, 59, 60, and 61 as they are overly broad and irrelevant and an invasion of the right to privacy of other persons without good cause.
The motion s granted as to Nos. 36, 37, 38, 39, 40, and 49 limited to San Francisco stores for the period of November 2003 to present, and limited to an identification of documents, without the identify of the individuals there is no good cause.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 53, 63, 85, 86, 87, 88. 89, 90, 91, 92, 94, 95, 96, 97, 98, 99, 100, 101, 102, 103, 104, 105, 112, 113, 114, and 115 as irrelevant.
The motion is granted as to No. 93, limited to the stores located in San Francisco.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 106, 107, 108, 109, 110, and 111, limited to the San Francisco stores for the past five years.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied.
MODIFICATION:
Order modifified as to No. 49 to limit production to stores supervised by this supervisor (Pine Street and Market Street stores) COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
